wikitext:
'''<big>Giraffe Attack/Wade-Stone Variation/Byrd Attack</big>'''

'''3. Qg4?!'''

This move is interesting. Here White aims to take advantage of the Bishop move. Here Black has options:

'''3...g6''' Is by far the most played move. It moves the g-pawn to comfortable square & prepares for f5.

'''3...Bf8?''' This is the Ancient Defense. It undevelops the Bishop, & is just a joke alone.

'''3...Qf6''' This is called the Modern Variation. It aims for the Scholar's Mate (with Black) & develops the Queen as well.

'''3...Nf6?!''' Is the Old Variation. A bit too aggressive on Master Level.

title: Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Nc3/2...Bc5/3. Qg4
